overall_summary: elizaOS successfully released version 2.0.2, introducing a critical 'Owner+Agent' identity model and stabilizing cloud infrastructure. Technical focus has shifted to resolving high-memory overhead for modern Qwen architectures and fixing deployment failures on the Waifu economic agent launchpad.
key_facts: The project launched v2.0.2, implementing the 'Owner+Agent' identity architecture to standardize secure agent ownership.
PR #7936 restored the 'milady-tails' Linux variant, bringing back Tails-based security hardening and encrypted USB persistence.
PR #7828 promoted the develop branch to main, triggering a full cross-platform release including iOS native llama xcframework hardening.
A technical issue was reported on the Waifu platform where users received a 400 status error 'Operations and map are required' during flap uploads.
ElizaOS token migration from ai16z has officially ended, with the highest liquidity currently available on the Binance Smart Chain (BSC).
PR #7883 integrated the NEAR AI inference provider, adding OpenAI-compatible support for its discovery surfaces.
The planner-agent pipeline is currently leaking sub-agent control metadata (e.g., 'Routing Kind: QUESTION_FOR_TASK_CREATOR') to user-facing Discord channels.
PR #7197 introduced @elizaos/vault to provide secure, cross-platform secrets management across headless and desktop environments.
Testing on Intel Lunar Lake iGPUs revealed that Qwen2.5-14B-Instruct runs at only 13% efficiency due to sub-optimal kernel selection in ggml-vulkan.
open_questions: What is the ElizaOS roadmap for the coming months?
Can I have an invite code to create my own?
How can I interview developers who have built AI agents with wallet access for security research?
Should sub-agent control-metadata be stripped at the framework layer or fixed in the sub-agent reply contract?

twitter_news_highlights.claim: Launch of 'waifu', the first economic agent launchpad built by Sol the architect and an Eliza agent.

new_issues_prs.title: chore(release): develop → main (v2.0.2)
new_issues_prs.author: lalalune
new_issues_prs.significance: Triggers v2.0.2 tag and full cross-platform deployment.

new_issues_prs.title: Planner quality: explicit chat-recall requests pick SHELL over SEARCH_MESSAGES
new_issues_prs.author: NubsCarson
new_issues_prs.significance: Identifies critical leak of internal agent metadata and inefficient tool selection.

new_issues_prs.title: Restore packages/os/linux/variants/milady-tails
new_issues_prs.author: NubsCarson
new_issues_prs.significance: Reverts a deletion to maintain Tails-level security hardening for the OS variant.

overall_focus.claim: The focus has been on foundational hardening, ESM-first plugin alignment, and implementing the new universal identity model (Owner+Agent) across connectors.

discord_updates.summary: Community focus on recruitment for an onchain game project and introductions from developers specializing in RAG systems and autonomous pipelines.
Market analysis provided for BTC, SOL, and XRP, noting institutional inflows into XRP ETFs and whale rotation as the week's theme.

user_feedback.feedback_summary: Users reported inability to finish the token migration as the team has officially closed the migration process.
Technical frustration regarding 400-status errors when attempting to use the 'waifu' launchpad for agent deployment.

strategic_insights.insight: Current modern Qwen architectures (3.5/3.6) are effectively unusable on specified hardware classes until M-RoPE awareness and SWA partial-sequence-removal are implemented in the spec-decode harness.
implications_or_questions: Does our current model recommendation engine warn users about these performance cliffs?

market_analysis.observation: XRP institutional accumulation and ETF inflows are driving a push toward the 2.00-2.35 resistance range.
market_analysis.relevance: High liquidity and institutional interest in assets within the elizaOS ecosystem (BSC/SOL/XRP) reflects growing project relevance.
